sections.subject: English

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course includes the following topic(s): Reading Level 3, Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 3, Written Narration: Grade 3

About Reading Level 3
The Alveary Reading Level 3 lessons feature captivating folk tales, fairy tales, fables, and wonder tales from around the world. The Free and Treadwell Reader for this course also includes the poetry of Robert Louis Stevenson and Christina Rossetti. By this Level, readers will typically have mastered basic word decoding. They will continue to practice higher-level phonics, learn less common spelling patterns, and investigate the morphology and etymology of new words. Fluency (the ability to read text quickly, easily, and accurately) is emphasized. Grammar concepts introduced in Levels 1 and 2 will be taught in more detail and new topics will be added. The intent of the lessons is that they serve as a bridge to independent reading by providing students with the capability for becoming self-directed, insightful, thinking readers.

About Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 3
Copy short sentences and write them from dictation.

About Written Narration: Grade 3
Students begin writing narrations or a part of a narration as appropriate.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Reading Level 3
Students who can already read may use the stories for reading practice but focus mainly on the spelling and grammar elements, including copywork and dictation. 

Prerequisite: Beginning Reading & Lang. Level 2 or appropriate skill proficiency.

Written Narration: Grade 3
Students who are not yet secure with their penmanship are not yet ready for written narration. Instead of written narrations, use the time to have students work on skills that will better support their development.

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Reading Level 3
☐  Print on cardstock, cut apart, sort, and label all Supplementary Materials for each lesson, found in the Introduction section of the Reading Lessons Teacher Guide. This should be completed before starting lessons with students. This is a crucial step needed in order to be successfully prepared for lessons.

☐ Continue entering lists of words that follow specific spelling or sound patterns in students' Word Journals.
 
☐ Equip students with blank-lined journals divided into sections for Copywork, Studied Dictation, and Written Narrations.

Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 3
If you are using the Getty-Dubay Italic Handwriting Series, complete half a page per day or go at student's pace.

Written Narration: Grade 3
☐ Aim to complete one written narration every week by the end of the year, but start with one every few weeks or every other week.
